verbatim: Preserve negation tokens during evidence-overlap validation.
action: Remove 'not'/'no' from _EVIDENCE_STOPWORDS (backend/services/ghost_b.py:682-684) or exempt negation tokens inside _evidence_token_overlap/_validate_evidence (ghost_b.py:690,715); add an asserting unit test where a relation whose evidence hinges on a negation token survives overlap validation with the token intact.
why: Stripping negation during evidence checks lets 'X does not cause Y' validate as 'X causes Y', poisoning graph promotion and typed-relation answers.
feasibility_note: Registry (registry_extraction.json, 'negation' row) pins THE defect to ghost_b.py:682-684 where 'not','no' sit in _EVIDENCE_STOPWORDS. Terminology: 'negation' collides with shipped RETRIEVER-side negated-query-concept filtering (_negated_concept_terms, query_plan.py:387; vocabulary.py 'negated_query_concept') — this claim is the EXTRACTION-side sense, canonical 'relation negation capture', which is PLAN-ONLY today.
required_receipt: pytest backend/tests green including a new test asserting a negation-bearing evidence phrase passes _validate_evidence with negation tokens counted in overlap; grep shows 'not'/'no' no longer silently dropped in the evidence-gate path.

verbatim: Add `negated` and evidence-sentence boundaries to the relation artifact.
action: Add `negated: bool` and evidence-sentence boundary offsets to RelationItem (backend/services/ghost_b.py:1988) and LLMRelation (ghost_b_schemas.py:137), bump ghost_b_extractions schema_version (polymath.extract.vN+1), extend GHOST_B_EXTRACTIONS_SCHEMA validator, and co-schedule with the P2.5 signature annotation so the relations[] artifact migrates once (Standing Rule 4).
why: Without a durable negated flag, promotion policy (L984) and temporal/graph consumers cannot distinguish asserted from denied relations.
feasibility_note: Registry confirms RelationItem today has NO negated field and no sentence boundaries ('relations' row: 'no negated field, no sentence boundaries'). 'Relation artifact' canonically means the ghost_b_extractions row, NOT the Neo4j edge (registry collision note). Standing Rule 4 (one migration per seam) requires riding the same artifact bump as P2.5's signature_valid annotation — do not migrate relations[] twice.
required_receipt: UGO canary extraction run writes ghost_b_extractions rows with relations[].negated (bool) + evidence-sentence boundary fields under a bumped schema_version; schema validator accepts new shape and rejects old-shape writes; asserting test file green.

verbatim: Parse only emitted relation evidence sentences unless benchmarks justify full-document parsing.
action: Implement sentence-scoped dependency/negation parsing over ONLY the emitted evidence_phrase sentences (worker already segments via spaCy, runpod_flash_extractor/app.py:292-299); write a benchmark comparing evidence-sentence-only vs full-document parsing on the UGO canary + a mark sample (latency, negation recall) before ever enabling full-document mode.
where: runpod_flash_extractor/app.py (spaCy parse scope), backend/services/ghost_b_local.py (local lane), benchmark script in backend/scripts/
why: Full-document parsing multiplies burst compute cost (pods bill by time) for negation signals that live in the evidence sentence.
feasibility_note: spaCy exists ONLY in the RunPod worker; the local GLiREL lane does its own windowing without spaCy (registry 'spaCy' row) — 'parse only emitted evidence sentences' therefore needs a per-lane answer, not one implementation. Default 'blank:en' pipeline has no parser, so negation-dependency parsing requires a real pinned model (ties to L983).
required_receipt: Benchmark artifact (script + numbers) showing evidence-sentence-only parse cost and negation-detection recall vs full-document on canary+sample; default config set to sentence-only with the benchmark cited in the checklist receipt.

verbatim: Ship and pin any required spaCy model in the RunPod contract.
action: Extend the EXISTING pin seam: RunpodFlashExtractionSettings.spacy_pipeline (backend/models/schemas.py:641, default 'blank:en', already forwarded per-request at runpod_flash_extraction.py:551) — select and pin a real spaCy model+version (e.g. en_core_web_sm==x.y.z) in the worker image deps (runpod_flash_extractor/app.py:538-546) and stamp it in the wire contract; add a contract test asserting pipeline name+version round-trip.
who: backend service (settings/wire contract) + RunPod worker image build
why: An unpinned parser model makes negation/sentence-boundary outputs non-reproducible across worker rebuilds, breaking parity measurement.
feasibility_note: PARTIALLY BUILT already: the pin FIELD exists and is forwarded per request (registry 'spaCy' row: 'the pin field exists'), but the default is 'blank:en' (segmentation only, no parser) — the open work is choosing/shipping a real model in the worker image, not creating the pin mechanism. Do not add a second pin field.
required_receipt: Worker image manifest/pip freeze shows the pinned spaCy model+version; wire request carries the pinned spacy_pipeline; test_runpod_flash_extraction asserts the pin; one canary batch returns sentence boundaries produced by the pinned model.

verbatim: Decide promotion policy for negated relations; retain evidence for audit.
action: Owner decides: are negated relations promoted to Neo4j (flagged), quarantined in ghost_b_extractions only, or dropped-from-graph-kept-in-artifact. Draft the options memo with per-option retrieval consequences from L992-style counts, record the decision in the checklist, then implement in graph_promotion_jobs honoring it; evidence rows retained regardless.
why: Promoting denied relations as positive edges corrupts graph-tier answers; silently dropping them loses contradiction evidence the answerability gates need.
feasibility_note: Genuine policy fork (promotion semantics), so 'owner' is correct here. Blocked on L980 (the negated flag must exist before a policy can act on it). Evidence retention is already the artifact-store norm (ghost_b_extractions is the durable store, graph_promotion_jobs.py:264).
required_receipt: Checklist entry recording the chosen policy + date; a promotion run on canary showing negated relations handled per policy (e.g. Neo4j edge count excludes/flags them) while ghost_b_extractions retains the rows.

verbatim: Build a reviewed predicate domain/range compatibility table from accepted real edges.
action: Do NOT green-field a new table: evolve the EXISTING hand-authored DOMAIN_RANGE_MAP (backend/services/ghost_b.py:289) into the reviewed table by mining accepted real edges (Neo4j promoted edges + validation_status counters) per predicate, and record review provenance for each row.
who: junior-agent (mining script) + owner review of the resulting table
where: backend/services/ghost_b.py (DOMAIN_RANGE_MAP), Neo4j promoted edges, backend/scripts/ (mining script)
why: A predicate signature table grounded in real accepted edges stops type-nonsense relations from reaching graph answers without over-rejecting valid ones.
feasibility_note: Registry flags HIGH duplicate-implementation risk ('typed signatures' row): the compatibility table ALREADY EXISTS under the domain_range name with enforcement (_apply_domain_range) and tests. The claim's wording 'build a ... table' is a non-canonical framing — canonical mechanism is 'domain/range validation (DOMAIN_RANGE_MAP + _apply_domain_range)'; implementing a parallel signature table would double the mechanism. Feasible only as an evolution of the existing map.
required_receipt: Diff showing DOMAIN_RANGE_MAP regenerated/extended from an accepted-edge mining script (script + input snapshot committed); review sign-off note per changed predicate row; existing test_domain_range_* suite green.

verbatim: Initially annotate `signature_valid` and violation reason; do not drop or remap edges automatically.
action: Demote the shipped remap behavior to annotation: change _apply_domain_range (ghost_b.py:2734) so mismatches stamp signature_valid=false + violation reason (retaining source_predicate) instead of remapping predicate to related_to; keep warn path; add tests asserting no predicate is rewritten.
why: Auto-remap destroys typed-edge information before violation rates are even measured, biasing the graph toward the related_to catchall.
feasibility_note: DIRECT CONTRADICTION with deployed behavior: current code remaps mismatches to related_to ('domain_range_mismatch', ghost_b.py:2734-2760) while the claim mandates annotate-only (registry evidence cites this exact conflict). Terminology: planned field name 'signature_valid' vs existing validation_status values 'domain_range_warn|domain_range_mismatch' — pick ONE surface (extend validation_status or add signature_valid) and document the mapping; do not carry both meanings silently. Rides the same relations[] migration as L980 (Standing Rule 4).
required_receipt: Test run proving a domain/range-violating relation retains its original predicate and carries signature_valid=false + reason; grep shows no remap-to-related_to path remains active by default.

verbatim: Measure violation rates by provider/model/corpus/predicate.
action: Extend the existing per-batch counters (domain_range_remap_count/domain_range_warn_count, ghost_b.py:2049-2050,2309-2310) into a persisted violation-rate report keyed by extractor engine, model ID, corpus_id, and predicate; emit per-burst diagnostics and a queryable rollup script.
why: Enforcement promotion (L993) is illegal without measured per-provider/predicate false-positive-relevant rates.
feasibility_note: Registry notes plan bullet 'partially built': batch-level counters exist but have no provider/model/corpus/predicate dimensions and no persistence. validation_status on relations[] already stores the per-row outcome, so the rollup is a read-only aggregation over ghost_b_extractions — cheap and non-destructive.
required_receipt: Report artifact (script output) showing violation rate broken down by extractor x model x corpus x predicate over at least the canary + one PoC-pair burst; numbers referenced in the L993 decision memo.

verbatim: Promote hard/soft enforcement only after false-positive review.
action: Hold until L992's report exists; then owner reviews sampled violations for false positives and decides hard (reject) vs soft (annotate+downrank) enforcement per predicate; implement only the decided mode.
who: owner (decision) + backend service for the decided enforcement
where: backend/services/ghost_b.py (_apply_domain_range enforcement mode), decision record in checklist
why: Premature hard enforcement on an unreviewed table silently deletes valid typed edges — the exact failure the annotate-first design prevents.
feasibility_note: Sequencing is internally consistent: L990 (annotate) -> L992 (measure) -> L993 (promote). Matches the hypothesis-promotion rule (promote only after live probes confirm rates against pre-set targets). Nothing to build now beyond the decision hook.
required_receipt: Decision record citing the L992 report + false-positive review sample; if enforcement enabled, an A/B or before/after run showing no valid-edge regression on the held-out suite.

verbatim: Version a shared extraction artifact contract across cloud, local, and RunPod engines.
action: Formalize the already-present versioning into one shared contract doc + code constant set: ghost_b_extractions.schema_version stamps (polymath.extract.v1/v2, ghost_b.py:1852,3391,3566; ghost_b_local.py:93) are already written by all engines — add the missing contract-hash and a single authoritative version registry so cloud/local/runpod cannot drift independently.
why: A versioned shared artifact contract is the precondition for honest engine-parity comparison (L1017) and for S11's one-time re-extraction.
feasibility_note: PARTIALLY BUILT: registry 'engine parity' row shows schema_version stamps + parity-by-construction (runpod adapter reuses ghost_b_local._to_results + SCHEMA_VERSION). Terminology: 'contract' is overloaded FOUR ways (wire contract runpod_gliner_relex.vN; embed contract runpod_embed.v1; durable ARTIFACT schema polymath.extract.vN; ExtractionContract engine-resolution) — this claim means the durable ARTIFACT schema (polymath.extract.vN); say so in the receipt to avoid closing it against the wire contract.
required_receipt: One module exporting the artifact schema version + hash consumed by all three engines (import graph proof); ghost_b_extractions rows from each engine on canary stamped with identical schema_version.

verbatim: Record extraction engine, model ID, contract hash, field-level methods, offsets, confidence, and evidence.
action: Gap-close the provenance field set: extractor + schema_version already recorded/backfilled (schema_validators.py:52-54; normalize_extraction_provenance.py); confidence + evidence_phrase exist on relations. Build the missing pieces: model ID per row, contract hash, field-level method stamps, and REAL char offsets (Audit Delta 2 verified entity char offsets are 0 everywhere — capture, not backfill).
why: Field-level provenance is what lets parity comparison and later audits attribute quality differences to a specific engine/model.
feasibility_note: Half-shipped (extractor/schema_version per registry 'provenance' row), half plan-only (contract hash, field-level methods). Offsets are a CAPTURE change requiring re-extraction to populate — they land for the PoC pair via the S11 burst, and polymath_v2 stays 0 under the freeze (owner directive); do not promise offsets corpus-wide.
required_receipt: Canary ghost_b_extractions row showing extractor, model_id, contract hash, per-field method, non-zero char offsets, confidence, and evidence_phrase together; validator enforces presence on new writes.

verbatim: Decide whether deterministic facts are supported per engine; do not make noisy facts mandatory for queryability.
action: Owner decides per-engine deterministic-facts support: FactItem exists in the shared dataclasses (ghost_b.py) but GLiNER/relex lanes do not emit facts — record which engines must emit facts[] and codify that queryability NEVER requires facts presence (no fact-count gate in readiness or retrieval).
why: Making noisy facts mandatory would block corpora extracted by fact-less engines from ever reaching queryable readiness.
feasibility_note: The artifact shape supports facts (ExtractionResult carries FactItem) but local/relex engines are entity+relation only — support is inherently per-engine, so this is a real policy decision, not a build gap. The guard-half ('do not make facts mandatory') is checkable today: no readiness/retrieval gate currently keys on facts, keep it that way.
required_receipt: Decision record listing fact support per engine (cloud/local/legacy_local/runpod_flash); grep/test proof that no readiness or retrieval gate requires facts[] non-empty.

verbatim: Leave `object_kind` blank when it cannot be grounded; do not fabricate it from coarse entity type.
action: Enforce blank-on-ungrounded object_kind: audit every writer of RelationItem.object_kind (provider-LLM lane ghost_b.py, local GLiNER pass-2 facet labelling via facet_tagger, relex worker) and remove any fallback that copies coarse entity type into object_kind; add a test with an ungroundable object asserting object_kind=='' .
why: Fabricated object_kind values poison typed-signature validation (P2.5) and downstream facet-aware ranking with false type evidence.
feasibility_note: object_kind is an existing RelationItem field (ghost_b.py:1988-1998). This is a negative-behavior guarantee, so the receipt must be an asserting test, not a code read. Interacts with L988/L990: signature validation must treat blank object_kind as 'unknown', not as a violation, or the annotate-only rule will mass-flag honest blanks.
required_receipt: Asserting test: ungroundable object yields object_kind=='' across all engines' conversion paths; signature validation treats blank as unknown (no violation stamp).

verbatim: Add relation-cue derivation only when source-evidenced.
action: Gate relation_cue population on source evidence: only derive relation_cue (RelationItem field) when the cue token/phrase is present in the evidence sentence; add validation that rejects cues absent from evidence_phrase.
why: Unevidenced cues are model hallucination dressed as provenance; they would corrupt future cue-based negation/typing heuristics.
feasibility_note: relation_cue already exists on RelationItem (registry 'relations' row lists it), so this is a validation rule at the existing evidence gate — same seam as L979, ride them together.
required_receipt: Test asserting a relation whose relation_cue does not appear in its evidence_phrase gets the cue blanked (or row flagged), and a source-evidenced cue survives.

verbatim: Keep deterministic alias/definition rules in shared backend validation.
action: Re-verify (this is a keep-invariant, mostly true today): alias/definition rules live in shared backend code (clean_alias, mine_acronym_pairs, add_identity_alias in backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py) and NOT in per-engine workers — grep runpod_flash_extractor/ and local_ghost_b/ for alias/definition logic and assert zero hits; add a CI grep-guard if any appear.
why: If alias/definition rules fork into engine workers, provider choice changes identity resolution and the query-translation contract silently diverges.
feasibility_note: Structurally true now: registry_alias.json places all alias admission/mining in corpus_lexicon.py (shared backend), and the worker deps (app.py:538-546) contain no lexicon code. The claim is a standing constraint on future P2.6 work rather than new construction — keep it green with a guard, not a one-time checkbox.
required_receipt: grep -rn 'alias\|definition' runpod_flash_extractor/ local_ghost_b/ shows no admission/normalization rules; alias behavior asserted identical across engines on one canary doc (same lexicon aliases regardless of extraction engine).

verbatim: Run the same post-extraction lexicon projector (co-occurrence, usage frames, semantic profile, DF/specificity, and representation admission) for cloud, local, RTX, and RunPod artifacts so provider choice cannot change the query-translation contract.
action: Keep the projector engine-blind and note what exists vs not: corpus_lexicon already reads ghost_b_extractions regardless of engine (parity-by-construction). The named projector features co-occurrence/usage_frames/semantic_profile/DF-specificity/representation-admission are P2.1/P2.2 PLAN-ONLY (grep = zero hits) and land in S5/S10 — when they land, they must land ONLY in the shared projector, never per-engine.
why: Provider choice must not change the query-translation contract — one projector over one artifact store is the parity guarantee.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY: 'RTX' is not an engine enum value — the ENGINES tuple is off/local/cloud/runpod_flash/legacy_local/dual (extraction_contract.py:15-19); 'RTX' colloquially means the legacy_local sidecar fleet on the RTX box (canonical: legacy_local engine). Also note usage_frames/semantic_profile are PLAN-ONLY fields (registry: grep = no hits; semantic_profile canonical = 'to-be-built P2.1 lexicon/card field'), so this claim cannot be fully closed before S5/S10 deliver those fields — close it as 'projector is engine-blind for all fields that exist'.
required_receipt: Same-doc extraction via two engines (legacy_local vs runpod_flash) followed by lexicon rebuild produces identical corpus_lexicon projections (field-for-field diff empty except extractor provenance); repeated after each new P2.1/P2.2 projector field ships.

verbatim: RunPod Flash contract, settings, backend adapter, and worker code exist.
action: Accept receipt — re-verified 2026-07-13 by registry probe: adapter (backend/services/runpod_flash_extraction.py:424 extract_entities, :131 _submit_and_wait, :281 _validate_wire_result), settings (backend/models/schemas.py:612 RunpodFlashExtractionSettings incl. model_revision pin), worker (runpod_flash_extractor/app.py), wire contract polymath.runpod_gliner_relex.v2/v3 with tests (test_runpod_flash_extraction.py, test_temporal_capture_contract.py).
why: These artifacts are the substrate every remaining P2.7 gate runs on.
feasibility_note: Registry 'RunPod' and 'wire contract' rows enumerate every symbol with file:line, generated 2026-07-13. Note the checkbox says 'exist' only — existence is honestly scoped; production-readiness is explicitly withheld by L1024.
required_receipt: cd /Users/king/polymath_v3.3 && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_runpod_flash_extraction.py backend/tests/test_temporal_capture_contract.py -q → exit 0.

verbatim: Deploy a pinned endpoint artifact.
action: Deploy the endpoint with a PINNED artifact end-to-end: worker image tag + model_id knowledgator/gliner-relex-large-v0.5 + model_revision 9c4171ae1e690fc29b87f33579e50bcd65faf2cc (already pinned in settings, schemas.py:631-641) + pinned spacy model (L983); verify the live endpoint rejects contract-version mismatch and record the deployed artifact identifiers in the checklist.
where: RunPod endpoint(s) incl. accounts[] endpoints, runpod_flash_extractor/ image, settings ingestion.runpod_flash.{model_id,model_revision,spacy_pipeline}
why: An unpinned endpoint makes every gate number (L1016) unreproducible — the artifact under test must be identified.
feasibility_note: The pin FIELDS all exist and are populated (model_revision default is a full HF sha; contract-version handshake with worker rejection is coded, app.py:23-28,546). Open work is the deploy+record act, not mechanism-building. Multi-account (P2.7c, shipped) means EVERY enabled account's endpoint must run the same pinned artifact or parity numbers fork per account.
required_receipt: Endpoint describe (per enabled account) showing the deployed image/artifact id + a canary request whose response echoes contract_version and pinned model identity; identifiers recorded next to the checklist box.

verbatim: Pass canary, 100, 500, and 5,000-chunk yield/quality/budget gates.
action: Run the graduated gates against the PoC pair (owner directive: NOT v2): canary (UGO), 100, 500, then 5,000-chunk burst, each recording yield (entities/relations per chunk), quality (evidence validation pass rate), and budget (cost vs budget_cap_usd=40, estimated vs actual) — knobs exist (benchmark_chunks=5000, budget_cap_usd, schemas.py:668-673). Precondition: owner signs DISPOSITION_MATRIX_2026-07-13.md (S3 artifact awaiting sign-off) and L1015's pinned artifact is live.
where: backend/scripts/ (gate harness), settings ingestion.runpod_flash, corpora UGO_CORPUS -> markbuildsbrands_transcripts + ecommerce_AI_FILM_SCHOOL
why: Graduated gates are the only path from 'code exists' to 'production-ready' (L1024) without betting a corpus-scale spend on an unmeasured lane.
feasibility_note: Feasible mechanically, but currently BLOCKED-ordered: the checklist's live P2.7b bullet-1 annotation says the disposition matrix is evidence-gathered but 'awaiting owner sign-off', and it recommends ecom = reingest SUBSET — running the 5,000-chunk gate on ecom before that sign-off could spend extraction on chunks slated for re-chunking (chunk IDs rebuilt = spend destroyed). Note the 2026-07-13 100-chunk run verified P2.7c ROUTING only, not these yield/quality/budget gates — do not double-count it. Silent-fallback accounting applies: preflight canary before each paid scale step.
required_receipt: Per-gate report artifact (canary/100/500/5000) with yield, quality, and cost-vs-budget numbers, run against the signed disposition scope, exit-0 asserting harness; 5,000-gate row cites the owner-signed matrix.

verbatim: Compare entity, relation, evidence, ontology, graph-promotion, and failure rates against current cloud/local paths.
action: Build the engine-parity comparison harness (none exists — grep 'parity' in backend returns only Mongo/Qdrant and embed-score senses): same chunk set extracted via cloud, legacy_local, and runpod_flash; compare entity/relation/evidence yields, ontology mapping rates, graph-promotion acceptance, and failure rates per engine.
why: Parity numbers are the acceptance currency for L1024 — without them 'production-ready' is a vibe.
feasibility_note: Registry 'engine parity' collision note is load-bearing: 'parity compare' here means engine-vs-engine yield/quality on the SAME chunks — distinguish from Mongo<->Qdrant ID parity (backfill_corpus_lexicon.py:516) and the runpod-EMBED cosine-parity gate (P1.9, 0.98) which are different 'parity's. Artifact shape is already shared (parity-by-construction), so the harness is aggregation + statistics, not plumbing. Local GLiNER+GLiREL vs remote gliner-relex are DIFFERENT model compositions (registry GLiNER row) — differences are expected and are exactly what must be measured, not assumed away.
required_receipt: Harness output comparing the 6 named rate families across ≥3 engines on an identical chunk sample (≥500 chunks), stored as an artifact and cited in the P2.7 acceptance row.

verbatim: Verify retries never erase valid summaries, vectors, or graph artifacts.
action: Write an asserting retry-safety test: snapshot summaries/vectors/graph counts for a canary doc, force a failed-then-retried extraction batch (inject worker failure), re-run, and assert byte/count-identical summaries, Qdrant vectors, and Neo4j artifacts (non-destructive snapshot+restore, run via docker exec per assert-before-commit rule).
who: script (asserting e2e test) via docker exec
where: backend/services/runpod_flash_extraction.py (retry/failover paths), mongo ghost_b_extractions + ghost_b_failures, Qdrant collections, Neo4j
why: A retry that clobbers paid summaries or vectors converts a transient worker failure into permanent data loss.
feasibility_note: Failover machinery exists (bounded failover per P2.7c; ghost_b_failures staging per registry) and extraction writes only ghost_b_extractions — summaries/vectors are different pipelines, so the invariant is likely already true BY CONSTRUCTION; the work is proving it with a non-zero-exit-on-fail test, not fixing code. Log-greps are not proof (feedback_assert_before_commit).
required_receipt: Self-asserting script (exit non-zero on any drift) showing pre/post-retry equality of summary rows, vector counts+ids, and graph artifact counts for the canary doc; run GREEN and cited.

verbatim: Wire extraction/concept readiness into corpus strict-readiness.
action: Add extraction-coverage and concept-coverage gates into the corpus readiness computation (backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py — fully_enriched already min()s against lexicon_ready, summary_tree_index_ready, document_profile_index_ready at lines 368-454; extraction/concept gates follow the same pattern), surfacing them under the S6 readiness split rather than overloading fully_enriched.
where: backend/services/ingestion/readiness.py, readiness API/diagnostics, docs strict-ready definition section
why: Without extraction/concept gates a corpus can read 'ready' while its graph/lexicon lanes are empty, making tier-3 answers silently degrade.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY: 'corpus strict-readiness' — canonical strict_ready is PLAN-ONLY (registry_readiness: no code symbol; the shipped meaning is corpus_readiness/fully_enriched). This claim therefore depends on S6 (readiness split: operational vs metadata-quality vs temporal capability) landing first or riding together; wiring extraction gates into fully_enriched directly would repeat the overloading S6 exists to fix.
required_receipt: Readiness endpoint for a corpus with known extraction gaps reports the extraction/concept gate as failing while operational readiness stays truthful; strict_ready definition doc lists the new gates; asserting test green.

verbatim: RunPod is called production-ready only after corpus-scale measured parity.
action: Hold as the P2.7 acceptance arbiter: 'production-ready' may be stamped ONLY when L1016 gate reports + L1017 parity harness numbers exist at corpus scale (5,000-chunk PoC-pair burst) and are cited; any earlier production claim is a ledger violation.
why: This is the anti-silent-fallback gate: it converts 'the code exists' into 'the lane is measured at scale'.
feasibility_note: Acceptance criterion, not work: it composes L1016+L1017 receipts. Consistent with the hypothesis-promotion rule (numeric targets set in advance). Nothing can close it early; treat as the terminal check of the S11 extraction program.
required_receipt: Checklist acceptance box checked with links to the 5,000-chunk gate report AND the engine-parity comparison at corpus scale; both artifacts dated and reproducible.

section: Active PoC Scope (owner directive 2026-07-13 — grounding for all testing)
verbatim: Designated proof-of-concept corpora: `markbuildsbrands_transcripts` and `ecommerce_AI_FILM_SCHOOL` — all cross-corpus retrieval testing, ingestion experiments, extraction bursts, and librarian/shelf iteration target these two until the owner widens scope.
action: Accept receipt (owner directive 2026-07-13, recorded verbatim in the checklist Active PoC Scope block) — enforce continuously: every test/experiment/burst in this chunk targets markbuildsbrands_transcripts + ecommerce_AI_FILM_SCHOOL until the owner widens scope.
who: owner (directive) — enforcement by every executing agent
why: A stable small PoC pair keeps iteration cheap and makes cross-corpus eval interpretable (film craft x marketing bridge material).
feasibility_note: Decision, not work; matches PLAN_CRITIQUE ground rules line 5 verbatim. Corpus names match registry_leftover canonical spellings (markbuildsbrands_transcripts; ecommerce_AI_FILM_SCHOOL).
required_receipt: Directive text present in checklist Active PoC Scope; spot-check that no P2.x job since 2026-07-13 targeted a non-PoC corpus (job logs / ghost_b_extractions timestamps).

section: Active PoC Scope (owner directive 2026-07-13 — grounding for all testing)
verbatim: `polymath_v2` is LEFT ALONE for heavy operations (its P0.1 repair is complete and verified; sheer size makes it wrong for PoC iteration): no re-extraction, no reingest, no mass backfills without a new owner directive. Read-only participation in the held-out eval suite continues.
action: Accept receipt and enforce: polymath_v2 gets no re-extraction/reingest/mass backfill without a NEW owner directive; P0.1 completion is independently receipted (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2: verify PASS all 4 corpora, exit 0, 2026-07-13). Read-only eval participation continues.
who: owner (directive) — enforcement by every executing agent
why: Freezing the 130k-parent corpus prevents accidental five-figure-chunk spends during PoC iteration.
feasibility_note: The embedded fact-claim ('P0.1 repair is complete and verified') is CONFIRMED by the plan critique's V-labeled probe (p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify, PASS all four corpora, 2026-07-13; the checklist's two IN PROGRESS tags were called stale). Freeze consistent with S2/S4 carve-outs: v2 still receives documents-only deterministic backfills (498 rows) and the deterministic temporal classifier — those are inside the freeze by plan design, don't misread this claim as banning them.
required_receipt: docker exec backend python p0_1_summary_integrity.py verify → exit 0 (re-runnable); absence of any v2 mass re-extraction/reingest jobs since directive (job collections timestamp check).

section: Active PoC Scope (owner directive 2026-07-13 — grounding for all testing)
verbatim: `UGO_CORPUS` stays the 1-document canary (first target for any new pipeline before the PoC pair).
action: Accept receipt (owner directive): UGO_CORPUS (1 document, 203 parents, 659 children — verified in DISPOSITION_MATRIX census) remains the first target for any new pipeline before touching the PoC pair; encode as the default canary in gate harnesses (L1016).
who: owner (directive) — harness default set by junior-agent

Owner must sign the audited matrix; the 5,000-chunk burst gate then runs against the SIGNED scope (T-HOOK-1 already landed, unblocking the 'pending T-HOOK-1' condition).

section: P2.7b RunPod Burst Orchestration (owner design, adopted 2026-07-13)
verbatim: Per-corpus disposition matrix BEFORE any mass job: each active corpus classified `reingest` (re-parse/re-chunk — bad heading_path/OCR; rebuilds chunk IDs so summaries/vectors/extractions follow) vs `re-extract-only` vs `projection-only`. No reindex or extraction spend on a corpus marked `reingest`.
action: Close the audit half, hold the gate half: the audit artifact EXISTS (docs/DISPOSITION_MATRIX_2026-07-13.md, deterministic classifiers, full censuses for ecom/mark/UGO, v2 sample, exit 0 — checklist bullet now annotated IN CODE awaiting sign-off). Remaining: owner sign-off (L1040), then persist the disposition as a per-corpus settings field (single source; render the sign-off table FROM it) and enforce 'no reindex/extraction spend on reingest-marked corpora' in the burst dispatcher.
who: owner (sign-off) + backend service (per-corpus disposition field + dispatch guard)
where: docs/DISPOSITION_MATRIX_2026-07-13.md; backend/services/ingestion/extraction_contract.py (natural home per registry), burst dispatch path in runpod_flash_extraction.py
why: The matrix is the spend-protection gate: it prevents paying for extraction over chunks that a reingest will delete.
feasibility_note: Registry 'disposition matrix' row: PLAN-ONLY as code (grep disposition = zero hits), gate artifact not config — and it flags risk (b): disposition could get encoded twice (sign-off doc + ad hoc engine toggles); make the corpus settings field the single source. The claims file's ledger_status 'open' is stale versus the live checklist annotation (evidence gathered 2026-07-13). Note the audit's finding that page_start needs a prov-capture CODE change — reingest alone will NOT add it; don't oversell reingest.
required_receipt: Signed matrix + a per-corpus disposition field readable via resolve_extraction_contract; asserting test: dispatching a burst against a reingest-marked corpus is refused with a diagnostic.

section: P2.7b RunPod Burst Orchestration (owner design, adopted 2026-07-13)
verbatim: Chunk-complete barrier: for a corpus ingest (e.g. 300 files), ALL valid children are parsed, metadata-extracted, and fully chunked BEFORE the first RunPod dispatch; extraction never interleaves with chunking.
action: Build the chunk-complete barrier in the ingest pass machinery: extend worker.py pass_plan / batches.py INGEST_PROFILES (which already sequence queryable-vs-enrichment passes) so a corpus-scope extraction pass becomes eligible ONLY when parse+metadata+chunking report complete for ALL valid children; barrier state must be durable (survives worker restart).
why: Interleaving extraction with chunking dispatches chunks that may be superseded, wasting paid worker-seconds and forking artifact lineage.
feasibility_note: No barrier code exists (registry 'burst orchestration' row: grep barrier/manifest = nothing relevant; nearest seam named = pass_plan/batches machinery). The runpod_burst profile currently sets 'summaries overlap, no endpoint pinning' — barrier semantics are additive to that profile, not a rewrite.
required_receipt: Asserting e2e test on UGO/mark: start a multi-file ingest, verify zero RunPod dispatches occur before the chunk-complete event, then the manifest dispatch begins; restart mid-ingest preserves the barrier.

section: P2.7b RunPod Burst Orchestration (owner design, adopted 2026-07-13)
verbatim: Saturating burst dispatch: build the full eligible-chunk manifest at the barrier, size request_batch_size x request_concurrency so queue depth keeps max_workers saturated end-to-end (pods bill by time — throughput IS cost), autoscale to awaiting-job volume, drain, then idle_timeout scales to zero. Record chunks/sec, worker-seconds billed, cost per 1k chunks.
action: Build the saturating dispatch layer over the shipped router: at the barrier, materialize the full eligible-chunk manifest, size request_batch_size x request_concurrency to keep max_workers saturated (knobs exist, schemas.py:612-690: request_batch_size=32, request_concurrency=8, max_workers, idle_timeout_seconds, scaler_value), autoscale to awaiting-job volume, drain to zero; persist per-burst chunks/sec, worker-seconds billed, and cost per 1k chunks in diagnostics.
who: backend service (runpod_flash dispatch) + script (burst metrics report)
why: Pods bill by time — an unsaturated fleet pays idle GPU seconds; the three recorded metrics are the budget-gate inputs for L1016.
feasibility_note: Half-shipped: multi-account least-in-flight routing + semaphores + failover EXIST (P2.7c live-verified); the manifest, saturation sizing, and the three cost metrics have NO code (registry 'burst orchestration' collision note b). Land dispatch changes in runpod_flash_extraction.py so the embed lane (which imports _AccountDispatcher/_submit_and_wait, embedder.py:581) inherits them — do not fork routing.
required_receipt: A 500-chunk burst report showing sustained worker saturation (queue depth ≥ max_workers until drain), scale-to-zero after idle_timeout, and recorded chunks/sec + worker-seconds + cost/1k-chunks; numbers reproduced in diagnostics endpoint.

section: P2.7b RunPod Burst Orchestration (owner design, adopted 2026-07-13)
verbatim: Burst runs the full local stack remotely: GLiNER-ReLEX + spaCy windows + deterministic python normalization in the worker; results return through the existing ontology/evidence/promotion gates only.
action: Reword before building — as written this is false-by-architecture: the RunPod worker runs GLiNER-ReLEX (joint entity+relation) + spaCy + deterministic normalization; the LOCAL stack is plain GLiNER + separate GLiREL (GLiREL is NOT shipped in the worker — app.py:538-546 deps have gliner+spacy, no glirel). Either (a) amend the checklist line to 'burst runs the RELEX stack remotely, artifact-parity measured via P2.7 compare' (recommended; matches parity-by-construction design), or (b) actually ship GLiREL into the worker (new work, new image, new pin). The 'results return through existing ontology/evidence/promotion gates only' half is true by construction (adapter reuses ghost_b_local._to_results into ghost_b_extractions).
who: owner (choose a vs b) then backend service/worker image
why: Pretending the remote stack equals the local stack would let a real model-composition difference hide inside 'parity assumed', corrupting every gate comparison.
feasibility_note: Registry GLiREL row states it directly: "'burst runs the full local stack remotely' (P2.7b) is currently false in the strict sense — the burst runs the RELEX stack, not the GLiNER+GLiREL stack." TERMINOLOGY: 'GLiNER' names two different models (local entity-only GLiNER vs remote gliner-relex joint) — canonical: distinguish plain GLiNER from GLiNER-ReLEX. This composition difference is exactly the parity surface L1017 must measure; option (a) is cheaper and epistemically honest.
required_receipt: Either amended checklist wording + L1017 parity report treating local vs relex as distinct engines, OR a worker image with glirel in deps + pipeline test proving GLiNER+GLiREL execute remotely with unchanged artifact shape.

section: P2.7b RunPod Burst Orchestration (owner design, adopted 2026-07-13)
verbatim: Sequencing: T-HOOK-1 (temporal capture in the wire contract) lands before the first corpus-scale burst; 5,000-chunk gate validates the burst profile itself (not just extraction quality).
action: Verify the landed half, hold the gate half: T-HOOK-1 IS landed — wire contract v3 additively carries time_expressions, renamed to temporal_captures + temporal_capture_version at the artifact boundary (runpod_flash_extraction.py:414-419, ghost_b.py:2088-2089, app.py:504-505; tests test_temporal_capture_contract.py:51-59). Re-verify via pytest, then ensure the 5,000-chunk gate (L1016) explicitly scores the BURST PROFILE (saturation, cost metrics from L1054) in addition to extraction quality.
who: script (pytest re-verify) + gate harness for the profile-scoring half
why: Sequencing exists so the one paid corpus-scale re-extraction carries temporal capture — running the burst without it would force a second paid pass (the exact C1 failure mode).
feasibility_note: The precondition is SATISFIED (checklist P2.7b row notes 'hook landed L'; registry 'wire contract' row verified the v3 fields). TERMINOLOGY: time_expressions (wire) vs temporal_captures (artifact) are the SAME concept renamed at _validate_wire_result — cite both when auditing. Remaining substance is making the 5,000 gate score the burst profile itself, which lands with L1016/L1054.
required_receipt: pytest backend/tests/test_temporal_capture_contract.py → exit 0 (hook re-verify); 5,000-chunk gate report contains a burst-profile section (saturation curve, cost/1k) distinct from extraction-quality metrics.

verbatim: Concept provenance and expansion-lane document hints exist.
action: Accept receipt with a fresh code probe (done today): concept provenance EXISTS as corpus_lexicon bounded source lists {source_document_ids, source_parent_ids, source_chunk_ids, alias_evidence} projected into Qdrant (qdrant_writer.py:1448-1462) plus source_document_support (corpus_lexicon.py:1818); expansion-lane document hints EXIST — vocabulary.py:1527-1540,2317-2446 consumes source_document_support/source_document_ids as document nominations, and grounded_planner.py:373 carries source_document_ids[:4].
why: This substrate is what the four open P2.8 items extend — its existence determines they are extensions, not green-field.
feasibility_note: Verified by live grep today AND by registry 'provenance' row which explicitly maps this checkbox to the lexicon source-ID mechanism. TERMINOLOGY: bare 'provenance' is a four-mechanism umbrella — P2.8's sense is the LEXICON source-ID lists, not extractor stamps (P0.8) nor the plan-only four-class taxonomy (P2.1/P2.2).
required_receipt: grep -n 'source_document_support\|source_document_ids' across corpus_lexicon.py/vocabulary.py/qdrant_writer.py reproduces the cited lines; one live query with expansion diagnostics showing document hints attributed to concept provenance.

verbatim: Extend provenance-backed routing to direct core-lane concept matches.
action: Extend the existing expansion-lane hint consumption (vocabulary.py:1527-1540) to DIRECT core-lane concept matches: when a lexicon concept matches the core/original query lane, its provenance-backed document nominations flow into routing with the same trust gates (_TRUSTED_EXACT_ALIAS_METHODS, vocabulary.py:55) — never bypassing the protected original-query lane.
why: Core-lane matches are the highest-precision concept hits; leaving their document provenance unused wastes the best routing signal the lexicon has.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY: 'core-lane' must be read against the lane-role vocabulary — protected lanes are role=='original' (retriever/__init__.py:1445-1447), and fusion distinguishes original vs core+required candidates (:2522-2528); canonical framing: extend hints from EXPANSION matches to ORIGINAL/CORE lane matches. Plan places P2.8 in S11 (after concept substrate improvements S5 refresh provenance quality). Guard: the non-negotiable invariant says concept hits may NOMINATE documents but cannot replace child/parent evidence.
required_receipt: Diagnostics on a held-out query showing a core-lane concept match contributing document nominations (new field in retrieval diagnostics), with original-lane protection metadata unchanged; A/B vs baseline shows doc-recall gain without original-lane displacement.

verbatim: Connect trustworthy concept provenance to final document-anchor protection.
action: Wire trustworthy concept provenance into final document-anchor protection THROUGH the single reservation discipline: route it via reservation_policy (P0.3 calibrated gate) so a provenance-backed document can hold a protected seat — do NOT add a fourth ad-hoc shield beside protected lanes, corpus reservation seats, and evidence_allocation protect_fn.
why: Provenance-trusted documents dropped at the final trim are the last-mile failure that turns correct concept routing into a wrong answer.
feasibility_note: Registry 'protected lane' row warns explicitly: P2.8's 'document-anchor protection' would be a FOURTH protect mechanism — 'wire it through reservation_policy's single-discipline contract, not a new ad-hoc shield.' TERMINOLOGY: 'anchor' here is sense (2) DocumentAnchorRetriever/document_anchor (title-recall lane + confidence gates), NOT P1.3 conversation anchoring (registry 'anchoring' row pins P2.8/checklist:1110 to sense 2). 'Trustworthy' must be defined via the L1091 gates before this can be enforced — sequencing dependency inside P2.8.
required_receipt: Asserting test: a document nominated by validated concept provenance survives final-packet trimming via a reservation_policy seat (metadata shows the reservation source); no new standalone protection code path introduced (code-review check).

verbatim: Require profile presence and validated source support.
action: Define and enforce the trust gate for L1088/L1089: a concept may route/protect documents only when (a) its lexicon entry has the required profile fields present and (b) source support is validated (e.g. source_document_support with count>=threshold across >=2 distinct documents — the >=3/>=2 pattern card_builder.py:134 already uses). Blocked in full until P2.1/S5 lands semantic_profile — gate on the fields that exist now (source_document_support, utility_gloss, provenance methods) and extend when semantic_profile ships.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (admission/trust gates), backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py (profile fields), later services touched by P2.1 semantic_profile
why: Without a presence+support gate, thin or junk concepts would reserve documents and the anchor protection (L1089) would amplify noise instead of signal.
feasibility_note: TERMINOLOGY AND DEPENDENCY: 'profile presence' most plausibly means the P2.1 lexicon semantic_profile — which is PLAN-ONLY (registry_latent: 'semantic_profile (to-be-built P2.1 lexicon/card field)', grep = zero code hits). Requiring presence of a field that exists nowhere would gate EVERYTHING off. Feasible now only in the reduced form (existing profile-ish fields + validated source_document_support); the full form waits on S5/P2.1. State which form is being closed.
required_receipt: Trust-gate code + test: concept lacking profile fields or validated support contributes zero document nominations/protections; diagnostics count gated-out concepts; receipt names the exact field set enforced (v1: existing fields; v2: + semantic_profile post-S5).

verbatim: Detect polluted concept cards that would mis-reserve documents.
action: Build a polluted-card detector (no code exists — grep 'polluted' in backend = zero hits): flag librarian cards/lexicon entries whose document support is dominated by the DF-worthless broad facet family (agency_preservation on 89,088 parents, emotional_patterns on 38,492 — 71-100% of every corpus, V) or by hub/junk concepts, and block flagged cards from document reservation until reviewed; align thresholds with the S7 owner decision on that facet family.
where: backend/services/librarian/card_builder.py, backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py, mongo librarian_cards + corpus_lexicon; S7 decision record
why: A polluted card mis-reserves documents at the exact point L1089 makes reservations powerful — detection must land with or before the protection.
feasibility_note: Green-field but well-scoped: the contamination signal is already quantified (PLAN_CRITIQUE section 2: broad-alias family stamps 71-100%, 'DF-worthless as a discriminator'; Audit Delta 2 confirms agentic_ai stamped on a film corpus). Interacts with S7 (owner DF-facet decision) and S8 (pair card rebuild) — detector should run as part of the S8 rebuild acceptance so freshly rebuilt PoC-pair cards ship clean. TERMINOLOGY: 'concept cards' = librarian_card.v0 (registry_librarian canonical), not lexicon entries — but pollution originates in lexicon/facet stamps, so detect at both layers.
required_receipt: Detector run over PoC-pair cards producing a flagged list with reasons (facet-family dominance / hub degree); asserting test that a flagged card cannot grant a document reservation; counts reported before/after the S8 rebuild.
